article.title: OSNO1: The Early Experimental Sound of Laura Les
article.subtitle: A foundational blueprint in the evolution of hyperpop
article.author: V. Reyes
article.excerpt: OSNO1 represents Laura Les's early experimental phase, characterized by glitchcore and digicore elements that laid the groundwork for her later work in 100 gecs.

content.text: OSNO1, the early solo project of Laura Les, serves as a crucial precursor to her later work with Dylan Brady in 100 gecs. This period is marked by raw, experimental electronic music that blends glitchcore and digicore influences, establishing a sonic blueprint for the hyperpop genre.

The OSNO1 sound is defined by its use of screeching synths, distorted vocals, and lyrics that evoke an internet-era aesthetic. Tracks such as 'A Conversation I Overheard' and 'I'm Sorry' exemplify this style, combining surreal narratives with glitchy, hypnotic production.

Before forming 100 gecs, Laura Les was crafting a unique universe of experimental electronic music under the OSNO1 moniker. This early work laid the foundation for the hyperpop movement, influencing a new wave of artists including Fraxiom, Jane Remover, and Gupi.

The OSNO1 era is characterized by a DIY digital punk energy, featuring pitched-up vocals and frenetic beats that capture the essence of early internet culture. This period represents a significant moment in the evolution of electronic music, marking the transition from experimental sounds to the hyperpop genre.
